title: Dolichandrone lutea
intro: Dolichandrone lutea, also known as the yellow bird of paradise, is a flowering tree species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Bignoniaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Dolichandrone lutea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the production of traditional remedies such as teas, tonics, and ointments.
taxonomy: Dolichandrone lutea is classified under the family Bignoniaceae. It belongs to the genus Dolichandrone and the kingdom Plantae. This plant is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Africa. It is a type of flowering plant that is known for its showy yellow flowers. Dolichandrone lutea is often found growing in woodlands, forests, and along riversides.
